evals.prompt: Find keyword cannibalization on example.com for United States/en, select the primary pages, recommend internal links, and save the report in the default location.
evals.expected_output: Runs the bounded DataForSEO MCP workflow, distinguishes same-intent competition from acceptable multi-URL rankings, assigns page decisions, creates a sampled internal-link map, and saves the detailed report under SEO/example.com/.
assertions: Relevant Pages, Ranked Keywords, Page Intersection, Instant Pages, and Content Parsing are used in the documented sequence.
Page Intersection uses no more than 20 priority pages and a maximum 1000-keyword union sample.
Every priority cluster identifies a primary page, competing pages, evidence, uncertainty, and one supported action.

evals.prompt: Check my site for pages competing with each other and fix the internal links.
evals.expected_output: Asks for the missing domain, country, and language and waits without making billable DataForSEO calls or writing a report.
assertions: The workflow requests domain, country, and language together.
No DataForSEO call is made until all three required inputs are supplied.

evals.prompt: Compare https://example.de/leistungen/seo/ and https://example.de/seo-agentur/ for Germany/de, discover other priority pages, and save the report under ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Prioritizes the supplied same-domain URLs, supplements them with discovered pages within the cap, verifies page content and link evidence, and writes an implementation-ready report under the requested root.
assertions: Supplied URLs are selected before discovered URLs and Germany/de is used consistently.
Canonicalization is reserved for duplicate pages that must remain reachable; redirect and differentiation decisions preserve distinct valuable intent.
Each internal-link recommendation includes source, target, natural anchor, context, and verified or unverified link status.
The report states that priority-page sampling does not guarantee a complete site-wide link graph.
